name: Tamil
register: Reverent contemporary Bible Tamil calibrated to the beloved Protestant Old Version tradition — பரிசுத்த வேதாகமம் (the 1871 Tamil Union / O.V. revision family that most Tamil churches read and quote from memory) — with its warmth eased toward the இந்திய சமகால தமிழ் மொழிபெயர்ப்பு (Indian Contemporary Tamil) where the O.V.'s literary Tamil would feel cold. The filial name draws on the O.V.'s own பிதா / பரமபிதா address (the Lord's-Prayer wording 'எங்கள் பிதாவே'), which a Tamil believer already hears as a child's word for their own father, never as the abstract தேவன்.
quote_convention: Double quotation marks “…” for speech, single ‘…’ for a quote nested inside; guillemets are not used in Tamil Scripture typesetting.
poetry_convention: Poetry (Psalms, Proverbs, prophetic oracles) set as broken indented verse-lines, one poetic colon per line; the second member of a parallel couplet is further indented so the parallelism is visible, matching O.V. poetic layout.

glossary.note: பரமபிதா ('supreme Father') is the O.V.'s own exalted-yet-paternal name; keeps the divine name filial rather than collapsing to பரலோக தேவன் ('God of heaven'). Vocative: எங்கள் பரமபிதாவே.

The primary filial divine name — the exact O.V. Lord's-Prayer address (vocative எங்கள் பிதாவே). NEVER தேவன் / கர்த்தர்; பிதா is the word a Tamil child uses for their own father, held reverent by tradition.

glossary.gloss: the (One who is) Father — honorific definite
glossary.note: Definite/honorific -ஆனவர் form gives 'the Father' as a titled person; distinct from anarthrous பிதா. Filial by stem, not தேவன்.
Same filial stem as 'our Heavenly Father' minus the possessive, for absolute address; chosen over பரலோக பிதா for the O.V.'s more devotional ring.
Retains the Aramaic அப்பா and glosses with the vocative பிதாவே — verbatim the O.V. rendering of Rom 8:15 / Gal 4:6, already the most intimate filial cry in Tamil Scripture.
REVERSION TOKEN — non-filial contrast term. O.V. word for God-as-God; use only where English keeps 'God', never as a substitute for பிதா. (Catholic/Common-Language alt: கடவுள்.)

REVERSION TOKEN for the covenant name YHWH (some editions யேகோவா). Not a substitute for பிதா. Note: O.V. also uses கர்த்தர் as the generic title 'Lord' for Jesus (Catholic tradition: ஆண்டவர்) — the clean Telugu split (యెహోవా vs ప్రభువు) is not lexically distinct in Tamil O.V.; reviewer to set the Jesus-'Lord' term.
Standard O.V. term; -ஆனவர் keeps the Spirit personal, not an it. Short form பரிசுத்த ஆவி in running text.
Relational genitive (பிதாவின் = of the Father), not by-identity; carries the பிதா filial stem, per the Genesis 1:2 preview vision.
Fixed O.V. Christological compound (மனுஷ + குமாரன்); the form Tamil readers recognize from the Gospels.
O.V. term for the divine Son; honorific குமாரனானவர் where 'the Son' stands titularly.
Traditional O.V. compound. Proposed — native reviewer to confirm vs the filial பிதாவின் குமாரன் ('Son of the Father'), parallel to the Telugu తండ్రి కుమారుడు question.
The exact O.V. John 3:16 phrase (ஒரேபேறான = sole-begotten); universally recognized, no need to modernize.
FHB-filial rendering of 'kingdom of God' — replaces O.V. தேவனுடைய ராஜ்யம் with the பிதா stem, per John 3:3,5 preview. (Contemporary alt அரசு available if reviewer prefers over ராஜ்யம்.)
Standard Tamil form across all traditions.
Standard O.V. transliteration; often இயேசு கிறிஸ்து as the full name.
O.V. transliteration used at John 1:41 / 4:25; kept distinct from கிறிஸ்து where the English marks 'Messiah'.
Standard, beloved Tamil Bible term for both Old and New covenant (புது உடன்படிக்கை = new covenant).
Renders hesed as loyal covenant love. Proposed — reviewer to confirm vs the O.V.'s beloved கிருபை ('lovingkindness/grace'), which carries the covenant-mercy sense but reads less as 'love'.

Natural noun for tender mercy. For the gut-level racham / splanchnizomai the O.V. uses the verbal மனதுருகி ('heart melting'); reviewer to confirm whether மனதுருக்கம் better fits the visceral register in narrative.
Standard O.V. term; நீதிமான் = 'the righteous one' where the person is meant.
The fixed O.V. John 3:16 phrase; instantly recognized. (Contemporary alt நித்திய ஜீவன் with space.)
From the O.V. John 3:3,7 wording 'மறுபடியும் பிறவாவிட்டால்' (unless born again); noun form மறுபிறப்பு ('rebirth') where a nominal is needed.
